title: triteleia ixioides var. scabra
intro: Triteleia ixioides var. scabra, commonly known as the rough purple lupine or scabrous triteleia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asphodelaceae family. It is native to the western United States and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The plant has been found to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to be used for its medicinal benefits, which include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting respiratory health.
